description: A 'wemari' is a person recognised as part of a group or institution under its membership criterion. Someone may 'wema' (belong) to a place without holding that status, while taking part in one meeting does not make a person a member. The group and the source of recognition complete the particular claim.
articulatory_notes: Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for voiced 'w', then glide into true-mid front 'e'. Close the lips for 'm', carry voicing through the nose, and release into stressed central 'a'. Tap or briefly trill the tongue tip before final high-front 'i'.
sound_symbolism: The word begins with intact 'wema' (belong): rounded 'we' enters the closed lips of stressed 'ma'. Final 'ri' adds a quick tongue movement and a brighter finish, as if belonging has received one further mark.
usage_notes: Members count individually, and the group commonly appears before the noun as a possessor. Admission and duration belong around the membership relation, as do suspension or dispute. 'pawela' (participate) reports taking part, while 'shalori' (represent) reports acting for another party.

examples.phi: ha sila wemari mua kowela pawela.
examples.translation: This community member participates in the council.

pillars.solarpunk-values: A commons can identify who shares its continuing work and decision relations without pretending that everyone nearby holds the same institutional status.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Membership leaves agreement and consent to the person's own speech, so a group label cannot supply their position.

semantic_domains.community: recognised participation in a group or institution under a stated criterion
